LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MELVIN A. POSADA' CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS DIRECTED TO RELEASE $2924.32 (WHICH REPRESENTS 25% OF THE TOTAL AMOUNT LEVIED) TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR FOR PAYMENT ON THE JUDGMENT. THE BALANCE OF THE FUNDS LEVIED SHALL BE RELEASED TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Disbursement Of Funds
Off Calendar Nov-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Donel R. Lopez, Esq. of Lepera + Associates, PC, T:415.362.2529, for plaintiff/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: Hearing held. Plaintiff's counsel informed the Court that the bond is separate from the funds that are at issue in the interpleader action and that Mr. Brereton has no objection to the motion that is before the court. Therefore, for good cause, and to the satisfaction of the Court,

PLAINTIFFs THE SIXTH AVENUE PARTNERSHIP, FRED TANG, WILLIAM TANG's Motion For Disbursement Of Funds is GRANTED.

Moving party to prepare and submit a proposed order by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Approving Receivers Final Report And Accounting Directing Payment Of Receivers Fees And Expenses Directing Distribution Of Remaining Funds Held By Receiver Discharge Of Receiver And Exoneration Of All Bonds
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mimi Chow, 415-830-1908, Tony Chow, 415-680-0818, Elaine Lee Chow, 415-680-8864 Defendants in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court addresses parties re: a signed stipulation, did not see an Opposition filed and did not receive an email contesting the matter. Defendant argues and contests $50,000 fee, interest. The Court tries to reach Mr. Bulmer on the phone but is unsuccessful. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

OTHER/COURT APPOINTED RECEIVER PATRICK BULMER's Motion For Order Approving Receivers Final Report And Accounting, Directing Payment Of Receivers Fees And Expenses, Directing Distribution Of Remaining Funds Held By Receiver, Discharge Of Receiver And Exoneration Of All Bonds.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Receiver Patrick Bulmer's motion for an order approving the Receiver's final report and accounting, directing payment of Receiver's fees and expenses, directing distribution of remaining funds held by Receiver, discharging Receiver, and exonerating the undertaking filed by Receiver on May 12, 2016 is granted. No opposition filed. The Court will enter the proposed order and the Stipulation and Order re Receiver's Determination of Claims. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
